name: Session Manager
description: Session lifecycle and persistence management for the Constrain CLI tool. Manages creation, saving, loading, resuming, listing, and completion of interview sessions. Each session is stored as a single JSON file under `.constrain/sessions/<id>.json`. Atomic writes (write to .tmp then rename) ensure Ctrl+C safety. Signal handling is delegated to the caller (engine/CLI catches KeyboardInterrupt and calls save). Overwrite confirmation for output artifacts is delegated to the CLI layer; this component only reports conflicts. Phase transitions are validated via an explicit allowed-transitions map.

types.name: Phase
types.description: The current phase of a session's lifecycle. Transitions are validated: understand→challenge→synthesize→complete, and any non-complete phase→abandoned.

types.name: MessageRole
types.description: Role of a message in the conversation history.

types.name: Message

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the message sender.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The text content of the message.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the message was recorded.

types.description: A single message in the conversation history.

types.name: ProblemModel

fields.name: domain
fields.description: The problem domain or area.

fields.name: core_problem
fields.description: Concise statement of the core problem being solved.

fields.name: key_entities
fields.description: Primary entities/concepts in the problem space.

fields.name: constraints
fields.description: Known constraints on the solution.

fields.name: assumptions
fields.description: Assumptions surfaced during the interview.

fields.name: quality_attributes
fields.description: Non-functional requirements and quality attributes.

fields.name: scope_boundaries
fields.description: What is explicitly in and out of scope.

fields.name: open_questions
fields.description: Unresolved questions that remain after the interview.

fields.name: topics_covered
fields.description: High-level topics that have been discussed.

types.description: Structured representation of the engineer's problem as understood through the interview. Fields are incrementally populated via problem_model_update JSON blocks from LLM responses.

types.name: RoundCounters

fields.name: understand_rounds
fields.description: Number of rounds completed in the understand phase.

fields.name: challenge_rounds
fields.description: Number of rounds completed in the challenge phase.

fields.name: understand_limit
fields.description: Maximum rounds allowed in the understand phase.

fields.name: challenge_limit
fields.description: Maximum rounds allowed in the challenge phase.

types.description: Tracks the number of conversation rounds per phase and enforced limits.

types.name: Session

fields.name: id
fields.description: UUID v4 string uniquely identifying this session.

fields.name: phase
fields.description: Current phase of the session.

fields.name: posture
fields.description: Name of the selected interviewer posture (e.g., 'socratic', 'adversarial'). Stored as name string, not full definition.

fields.name: problem_model
fields.description: The incrementally-built problem model.

fields.name: round_counters
fields.description: Round tracking and limits per phase.

fields.name: conversation_history
fields.description: Full conversation history across all phases.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of session creation.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last session update.

fields.name: completed_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of session completion. Empty string if not yet complete.

types.description: Complete session state. Serialized as a single JSON file. Contains all data needed to resume or complete a session.

types.name: SessionSummary

fields.name: id
fields.description: UUID v4 string of the session.

fields.name: phase
fields.description: Current phase of the session.

fields.name: posture
fields.description: Name of the interviewer posture.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of session creation.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last update.

fields.name: total_rounds
fields.description: Total number of conversation rounds across all phases.

fields.name: topics_covered
fields.description: High-level topics discussed, extracted from the problem model.

fields.name: is_complete
fields.description: Whether the session is in 'complete' or 'abandoned' phase.

types.description: Lightweight session metadata for listing purposes. Does not include conversation history to avoid loading large files.

types.name: ResumeSummary

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: UUID of the session being resumed.

fields.name: phase
fields.description: Current phase at time of resume.

fields.name: posture
fields.description: Posture name for the session.

fields.name: current_round
fields.description: Current round number in the active phase.

fields.name: round_limit
fields.description: Maximum rounds allowed for the active phase.

fields.name: core_problem
fields.description: The core problem statement if identified, empty string otherwise.

fields.name: topics_covered
fields.description: Topics covered so far.

fields.name: open_questions
fields.description: Outstanding questions.

fields.name: last_active
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the session was last active.

types.description: Summary information displayed when resuming a session, providing context without replaying history.

types.name: PhaseTransition

fields.name: from_phase
fields.description: The phase being transitioned from.

fields.name: to_phase
fields.description: The phase being transitioned to.

types.description: Represents an allowed phase transition with metadata.

types.name: SessionError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

types.description: Base error type for all session-related errors. Provides human-readable error messages, not stack traces.

types.name: SessionNotFoundError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: The session ID that was not found.

types.description: Raised when a session with the specified ID does not exist on disk.

types.name: SessionCorruptedError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: The session ID that is corrupted.

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: Path to the corrupted file.

types.description: Raised when a session file exists but cannot be parsed or fails validation.

types.name: SessionStorageError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: path
fields.description: The filesystem path where the error occurred.

types.description: Raised when file I/O operations fail (permissions, disk space, etc.).

types.name: InvalidPhaseTransitionError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

fields.name: from_phase
fields.description: The current phase.

fields.name: to_phase
fields.description: The target phase that was rejected.

types.description: Raised when an attempted phase transition is not in the allowed-transitions map.

types.name: GitignoreStatus
types.description: Result of checking whether .constrain/ is covered by .gitignore.

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Construct a SessionManager instance. Stores the base path under which `.constrain/sessions/` will be created. Does not create directories at construction time.

inputs.name: base_path
inputs.description: The root project directory path. Session files will be stored under `<base_path>/.constrain/sessions/`.

preconditions: base_path is a valid filesystem path string
postconditions: SessionManager instance is initialized with base_path stored
No directories are created on disk at this point

functions.name: create
functions.description: Create a new session with a generated UUID v4 ID, selected posture, empty problem model, and phase set to 'understand'. Posture is selected randomly from available postures unless overridden via the posture_override parameter or the CONSTRAIN_POSTURE environment variable (parameter takes precedence over env var). The session is NOT automatically saved to disk; the caller must call save() explicitly.

inputs.name: posture_override
inputs.description: Optional posture name to use instead of random selection. If empty string or not provided, checks CONSTRAIN_POSTURE env var, then falls back to random selection.

error_cases.name: invalid_posture
error_cases.condition: posture_override (or env var) specifies a posture name that is not in the known posture set

postconditions: Returned Session has a valid UUID v4 as its id
Session.phase == 'understand'
Session.problem_model has all fields at default (empty) values
Session.conversation_history is an empty list
Session.round_counters has all round counts at 0
Session.created_at and Session.updated_at are set to current time in ISO 8601
Session.completed_at is empty string
If posture_override is non-empty, Session.posture == posture_override
If posture_override is empty and CONSTRAIN_POSTURE env var is set, Session.posture == env var value
Session is not yet persisted to disk

structured_side_effects.description: Pure creation — does not write to disk or read files. Only reads CONSTRAIN_POSTURE env var.

functions.name: save
functions.description: Persist session state to `.constrain/sessions/<id>.json`. Uses atomic write strategy: writes to a temporary file (`<id>.json.tmp`) then renames to final path, ensuring Ctrl+C safety. Creates `.constrain/sessions/` directory structure if it does not exist. On the first save (when the sessions directory is first created), checks for `.constrain/` coverage in .gitignore and prints a suggestion if missing. Updates Session.updated_at to current time before writing.

error_cases.name: storage_write_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to write to the sessions directory due to permissions, disk space, or other I/O errors

error_cases.name: directory_creation_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to create `.constrain/sessions/` directory

preconditions: session.id is a valid UUID v4 string
session is a valid Session instance with all required fields
postconditions: File exists at `<base_path>/.constrain/sessions/<session.id>.json`
File contains valid JSON that can be deserialized back to a Session
No .tmp file remains on disk after successful save
session.updated_at has been updated to the current time
`.constrain/sessions/` directory exists
side_effects: Writes session JSON to `.constrain/sessions/<id>.json` via atomic tmp+rename
May create `.constrain/sessions/` directory if it does not exist
May print .gitignore suggestion to stdout on first directory creation

structured_side_effects.description: Checked on first save to suggest adding .constrain/ entry
Prints .gitignore suggestion if .constrain/ is not covered

functions.name: load
functions.description: Load a session by its UUID from disk. Reads and parses the JSON file, validates it against the Session Pydantic model. Returns a fully hydrated Session including conversation history.

inputs.name: session_id
inputs.description: UUID v4 string of the session to load.

error_cases.name: session_not_found
error_cases.condition: No file exists at `.constrain/sessions/<session_id>.json`

error_cases.name: session_corrupted_invalid_json
error_cases.condition: File exists but contains invalid JSON

error_cases.name: session_corrupted_invalid_schema
error_cases.condition: File contains valid JSON but fails Pydantic model validation

error_cases.name: storage_read_failure
error_cases.condition: File exists but cannot be read due to permissions or I/O error

preconditions: session_id is a valid UUID v4 string
postconditions: Returned Session has all fields populated from the persisted JSON
Session.id == session_id
Session.phase is a valid Phase enum value
All nested models (ProblemModel, RoundCounters, Messages) are valid

structured_side_effects.description: Reads session JSON file from disk

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is conversation history length

functions.name: find_latest_incomplete
functions.description: Scan all session files and return the most recently updated session that is not in 'complete' or 'abandoned' phase. Used for the resume workflow. Returns None if no incomplete sessions exist. Loads only metadata from each file (optimized to avoid full deserialization where possible, but falls back to full load if needed).

error_cases.name: storage_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to read the sessions directory or individual session files

error_cases.name: no_sessions_directory
error_cases.condition: The `.constrain/sessions/` directory does not exist — no sessions have ever been created

error_data.message: No sessions directory found. Start a new session with 'constrain start'.

postconditions: If a Session is returned, its phase is one of: 'understand', 'challenge', 'synthesize'
If a Session is returned, it has the most recent updated_at among all incomplete sessions
If None is returned, no incomplete sessions exist on disk

structured_side_effects.description: Scans directory listing and reads session JSON files

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of session files on disk

functions.name: get_resume_summary
functions.description: Generate a ResumeSummary for a session, extracting key context information to display to the user when resuming. Shows current phase, round number, core problem, topics covered, and open questions without replaying conversation history.

inputs.name: session
inputs.description: The session to summarize for resume.

error_cases.name: session_already_complete
error_cases.condition: Session is in 'complete' or 'abandoned' phase and cannot be resumed

error_data.message: Cannot resume a session in '{phase}' phase.

postconditions: ResumeSummary.session_id == session.id
ResumeSummary.phase == session.phase
ResumeSummary.current_round matches the round count for the active phase from session.round_counters
ResumeSummary.round_limit matches the limit for the active phase from session.round_counters
ResumeSummary.topics_covered == session.problem_model.topics_covered

functions.name: list_all
functions.description: Return lightweight summaries of all sessions. Reads each session file but constructs SessionSummary objects that omit conversation history. Sorted by updated_at descending (most recent first). Returns an empty list if the sessions directory does not exist or contains no session files.

error_cases.name: storage_read_failure
error_cases.condition: Unable to read the sessions directory due to permissions

postconditions: Returned list contains one SessionSummary per valid session file on disk
List is sorted by updated_at descending
Corrupted session files are skipped with a warning, not included in results

structured_side_effects.description: Reads all session JSON files for summary extraction
Logs warnings for corrupted session files that are skipped

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of session files

functions.name: transition_phase
functions.description: Transition a session from its current phase to a new phase. Validates the transition against the allowed-transitions map: understand→challenge, challenge→synthesize, synthesize→complete, and any of {understand, challenge, synthesize}→abandoned. Updates the session's phase and updated_at. If transitioning to 'complete', sets completed_at. Does NOT save to disk — caller must call save() after.

error_cases.name: invalid_transition
error_cases.condition: The transition from session.phase to to_phase is not in the allowed-transitions map

postconditions: Returned session's phase == to_phase
Returned session's updated_at is set to current time
If to_phase is 'complete', returned session's completed_at is set to current time
All other session fields remain unchanged
Session is not persisted to disk

structured_side_effects.description: Pure state mutation on the session object, no I/O

functions.name: update_problem_model
functions.description: Apply an incremental update to the session's problem model from a parsed problem_model_update dict (from LLM response JSON block). Merges list fields (appends new unique items) and overwrites scalar fields. Updates session.updated_at. Does NOT save to disk.

inputs.name: session
inputs.description: The session whose problem model to update.

inputs.name: updates
inputs.description: Dictionary of field_name→value pairs to apply. Keys must match ProblemModel field names. List-valued fields are merged (new unique items appended), string fields are overwritten.

error_cases.name: invalid_field
error_cases.condition: updates contains a key that is not a valid ProblemModel field name

error_cases.name: wrong_phase
error_cases.condition: Session is not in 'understand' or 'challenge' phase

error_data.message: Cannot update problem model in '{phase}' phase.

preconditions: updates contains only keys that are valid ProblemModel field names
postconditions: For each string field in updates, session.problem_model.<field> == updates[field]
For each list field in updates, all items from updates[field] that were not already present are appended to session.problem_model.<field>
Fields not mentioned in updates are unchanged
session.updated_at is set to current time

structured_side_effects.description: Pure state mutation on the session object, no I/O

functions.name: add_message
functions.description: Append a message to the session's conversation history. Increments the round counter for the current phase when an assistant message is added (a full round = one user message + one assistant response). Updates session.updated_at. Does NOT save to disk.

inputs.name: session
inputs.description: The session to add the message to.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: The role of the message sender ('user' or 'assistant').

error_cases.name: session_not_active
error_cases.condition: Session is in 'complete' or 'abandoned' phase

error_data.message: Cannot add messages to a session in '{phase}' phase.

error_cases.name: empty_content
error_cases.condition: content is empty or whitespace-only

postconditions: session.conversation_history has one additional Message at the end
The appended Message has role, content, and timestamp set correctly
If role is 'assistant' and phase is 'understand', session.round_counters.understand_rounds is incremented by 1
If role is 'assistant' and phase is 'challenge', session.round_counters.challenge_rounds is incremented by 1
session.updated_at is set to current time

structured_side_effects.description: Pure state mutation on the session object, no I/O

functions.name: is_round_limit_reached
functions.description: Check whether the current phase's round limit has been reached or exceeded. Returns True if the session should be prompted to transition to the next phase.
postconditions: Returns True if the current phase's round count >= the phase's round limit
Returns False otherwise

functions.name: check_output_conflicts
functions.description: Check whether prompt.md and/or constraints.yaml already exist in the specified output directory. Returns a list of conflicting file paths. The caller (CLI) is responsible for presenting overwrite confirmation to the user.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: Path to the directory where output files would be written (typically current working directory).

error_cases.name: invalid_directory
error_cases.condition: output_dir does not exist or is not a directory

preconditions: output_dir is a valid directory path
postconditions: Returned list contains Path strings for each of prompt.md and constraints.yaml that already exist in output_dir
Returned list is empty if neither file exists
Returned list has at most 2 elements

structured_side_effects.description: Checks existence of output files in the specified directory

functions.name: check_gitignore
functions.description: Check whether the .gitignore file in the specified project root contains an entry that covers `.constrain/`. Reads the .gitignore file and checks for patterns that would match `.constrain/` (exact match, wildcard patterns, etc.). Returns a GitignoreStatus enum value.

inputs.name: project_root
inputs.description: Path to the project root directory where .gitignore is expected.

error_cases.name: gitignore_read_failure
error_cases.condition: .gitignore file exists but cannot be read

preconditions: project_root is a valid directory path
postconditions: Returns 'covered' if .gitignore exists and contains a pattern matching .constrain/
Returns 'not_covered' if .gitignore exists but does not contain a matching pattern
Returns 'no_gitignore_file' if no .gitignore file exists at project_root

structured_side_effects.description: Reads .gitignore file to check for .constrain/ coverage

functions.name: delete_session
functions.description: Delete a session file from disk. Used for cleanup of abandoned or corrupted sessions.

inputs.name: session_id
inputs.description: UUID v4 string of the session to delete.

error_cases.name: session_not_found
error_cases.condition: No session file exists with the given ID

error_cases.name: delete_failure
error_cases.condition: File exists but cannot be deleted due to permissions

preconditions: session_id is a valid UUID v4 string
postconditions: No file exists at `.constrain/sessions/<session_id>.json`
If a .tmp file for this session existed, it is also removed

structured_side_effects.description: Deletes the session JSON file (and any .tmp file)

invariants: A Session.id is always a valid UUID v4 string and is immutable after creation
A Session.phase can only change through the allowed transitions: understand→challenge, challenge→synthesize, synthesize→complete, {understand,challenge,synthesize}→abandoned
A session in 'complete' or 'abandoned' phase cannot have its phase, conversation_history, or problem_model modified
Session.created_at is immutable after creation and always in ISO 8601 format
Session.updated_at is always >= Session.created_at and is updated on every mutation
Session.completed_at is empty string until phase transitions to 'complete', then set to current time and immutable
Round counters are monotonically increasing and never decrease
understand_rounds <= understand_limit and challenge_rounds <= challenge_limit (hard enforcement)
Each session file on disk is a valid JSON document deserializable to a Session model
Atomic writes ensure no partial/corrupted session files exist after save completes
The sessions directory path is always `<base_path>/.constrain/sessions/`
Session.posture is always a non-empty string matching a known posture name
ProblemModel list fields contain no duplicate entries after update_problem_model merges
Conversation history messages are append-only and never modified or removed
